summary: Tested Ember chat app end-to-end. Backend: 20/20 pytest tests passed (auth, users search, conversations CRUD, messages, websocket message delivery, typing, call signaling). Frontend: register -> chat creation -> real-time message exchange between two browser contexts -> typing indicator -> logout all functional. Only the call modal did not become visible after clicking audio-call-button (likely getUserMedia rejection in headless Chromium - non-blocking per request).

minor.issue: Returns messages BEFORE applying read_by update, so the just-read state is visible only on the next fetch. Acceptable but could be confusing for clients.

allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False - correct for Bearer auth path, but the backend also sets httpOnly cookies that the frontend cannot use cross-origin under this config. Cookies are effectively unused; consider explicit origins + allow_credentials=True if cookie auth is intended.

ui_bugs.issue: Audio/Video call modal did not become visible after clicking audio-call-button in headless test. Likely due to getUserMedia rejection in headless Chromium. Needs verification in a real browser; consider showing the modal even before media is acquired and surfacing a permission-denied state inside the modal so the user gets feedback.

issues: Decorative empty rectangle box above 'Pick a thread, or start one.' renders empty - intentional minimalism but might look like a missing image to first-time users

action_items: Verify call modal opens in non-headless browser; ensure modal renders before/independently of getUserMedia and shows a clear permission-denied state.
Decide on cookie vs Bearer auth: either remove set_cookie calls or set CORS allow_origins explicitly with allow_credentials=True.
(Optional) Reorder messages endpoint to update read_by before returning, then reflect updated read_by in the returned payload.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is single-file ~460 lines - acceptable but on the edge; consider splitting auth, conversations, messages, ws into modules.

WebSocket auth fails silently with await ws.close(code=4401) without ws.accept() first - per WS spec, server should accept then close, otherwise client may see generic handshake error rather than 4401.
Pydantic models lack validators on username (only length); allows whitespace, special chars - consider regex.
search_users uses raw $regex with user input - allows ReDoS / regex injection. Escape user input via re.escape before passing to $regex.
Messages endpoint has limit(500) but no pagination cursor - large conversations will be truncated silently.
test_credentials: alice@test.com/password123, bob@test.com/password123 (existing); plus dynamically registered TEST_alice_<uuid>/TEST_bob_<uuid> per run
seed_data_creation: Tests register fresh users with unique uuid suffix per run; no fixed seed data created.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py and are deterministic (uuid-suffixed users). WebSocket testing works via `websockets` lib at wss://<host>/api/ws?token=. For frontend, use two browser contexts to verify real-time. Call modal needs non-headless browser to fully verify.
rca of the issue: Call modal not visible: Almost certainly because the frontend awaits navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia before showing CallModal. In headless Chromium without --use-fake-ui-for-media-stream, the call rejects and the modal is never mounted. Fix: render the modal first (UI feedback), then attempt getUserMedia, and on failure show an inline 'Permission denied / no devices' state with an end-call button.
